tier_1.title: Ensure /tmp is configured
tier_1.vulnerability: Keeps /tmp in memory or isolated — prevents /tmp exhaustion from filling the root filesystem.

tier_1.title: Ensure nodev, nosuid, and noexec are set on /dev/shm
tier_1.vulnerability: Shared memory is a favourite attack staging area — noexec prevents in-memory execution attacks.

tier_2.title: Ensure mounting of cramfs is disabled
tier_2.vulnerability: cramfs is never used on modern systems — reducing loadable module attack surface.

tier_2.title: Ensure mounting of hfs and hfsplus is disabled
tier_2.vulnerability: macOS-only filesystems with no legitimate Linux workstation use — historical CVE exposure.

tier_2.title: Ensure mounting of jffs2 is disabled
tier_2.vulnerability: Flash filesystem — unmaintained upstream with known out-of-bounds vulnerabilities.

tier_2.title: Ensure mounting of udf is disabled
tier_2.vulnerability: UDF has a long CVE history including kernel panics and privilege escalation via malformed images.

tier_2.title: Ensure firewire-core module is disabled
tier_2.vulnerability: FireWire (IEEE 1394) enables DMA attacks — physical access can read/write all RAM without OS involvement.
